sub_claims.label: state_elimination

original_presence: spec.tla:85  — ce_archiver_ack_count declared in VARIABLES block
spec.tla:108 — ce_archiver_ack_count included in archiverVars tuple
spec.tla:151 — TypeInvariant: \A a \in CEArchivers : \A s \in 1..MaxSeq : ce_archiver_ack_count[a][s] \in Nat
spec.tla:695,697 — ArchiverGCByAcks reads and mutates ce_archiver_ack_count
spec.tla:634,648,661,675,687,714 — six UNCHANGED clauses list ce_archiver_ack_count
evidence.modified_archiverVars: spec_modified.tla:107-108 — archiverVars == <<ce_archiver_store, ob_archiver_store, ob_archiver_pending, oms_archiver_store>> — ce_archiver_ack_count removed, no new variable added
evidence.state_count: Init line 193 in original: domain is CEArchivers x 1..MaxSeq, so |CEArchivers| * MaxSeq integers. Eliminated entirely. Replacement state introduced: none.

sub_claims.notes: Variable fully excised from VARIABLES, TypeInvariant, archiverVars, Init, all UNCHANGED clauses, the action body, and P19. No replacement variable introduced.

sub_claims.label: idempotency_bug_fix

original_double_fire_trace: spec.tla:693 — only guard: s \in ce_backup_applied. This is a set membership test; ce_backup_applied is not modified by this action, so the guard remains true on every subsequent fire.
spec.tla:695-696 — ce_archiver_ack_count[a][s] increments unconditionally: no check whether GC already occurred or count already reached GC_K.
spec.tla:697 — GC fires when count+1 >= GC_K but counter continues to grow on each re-fire with no ceiling, inflating past GC_K.
Result: the action can fire arbitrarily many times for the same (a, s) after the GC already executed, with no enabling condition that becomes false.
modified_idempotency: spec_modified.tla:692 — watermark == MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied) (deterministic, no mutable counter)
spec_modified.tla:693-694 — enabling condition: \E e \in ce_archiver_store[a] : e.session = ce_primary /\ e.seq <= watermark
spec_modified.tla:695-696 — effect removes exactly those qualifying effects: {e \in @ : e.session /= ce_primary \/ e.seq > watermark}

After first fire: all current-session effects with seq <= watermark are gone. The existential at lines 693-694 finds no qualifying effect. Action is disabled until watermark advances (i.e., new effects ingested at higher seq and backup_applied grows). Natural idempotency via state exhaustion.

sub_claims.notes: Original bug confirmed at spec.tla:693-697. Modified version self-disables after first GC for any given (session, watermark) pair. No counter needed and no overflow possible.

sub_claims.label: session_scoping

evidence.original_filter: spec.tla:698-699 — {e \in @ : e.seq > s}. Session-blind: removes all effects with seq <= s regardless of which session produced them. An effect from a prior session with seq <= current watermark would be deleted.

filter_analysis.old_session_effects: e.session /= ce_primary is TRUE for old-session effects. First disjunct satisfied. Effect is KEPT unconditionally.
filter_analysis.current_session_above_watermark: e.session = ce_primary /\ e.seq > watermark: second disjunct TRUE. KEPT.
filter_analysis.current_session_at_or_below_watermark: e.session = ce_primary /\ e.seq <= watermark: both disjuncts FALSE. REMOVED (this is the GC target).

p19_alignment: spec_modified.tla:1003-1011 — ArchiverDAMonotonicity has three disjuncts for CE archiver effects:
(a) e.seq > ce_checkpoint_seq — not yet checkpointed, safe to hold
(b) e.session /= ce_primary — old-session effect, immune to watermark GC
(c) ce_backup_applied /= {} /\ e.seq > MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied) — current-session effect above watermark
Old-session effects satisfy disjunct (b) regardless of seq. They can only be removed by ArchiverGCByCheckpoint (spec_modified.tla:703-711), which is gated on ce_checkpoint_seq advancing past e.seq.
Note: P19 in original (spec.tla:1008-1012) used ce_archiver_ack_count[a][e.seq] < GC_K as the second disjunct — session-blind and tied to the eliminated counter.

sub_claims.notes: Session scoping structurally enforced by both the GC filter predicate and the revised P19. Old-session effects are preserved in ce_archiver_store until checkpoint path advances.

overall_notes: All three sub-claims verified against specific lines in both specs. The files are distinct: spec.tla has ce_archiver_ack_count in 13 locations; spec_modified.tla has 0. The structural delta is internally consistent: state removal is complete with no orphan references, the idempotency fix is mechanically sound via existential self-disabling, and session scoping is correctly expressed in both the GC filter and the updated P19 invariant.
